questions.question: Which ancient Indian dynasty built the city of Pataliputra?
questions.explanation: The Mauryan dynasty built the city of Pataliputra, which is now known as Patna, in the 3rd century BCE. It was the capital of the Mauryan Empire, which was founded by Chandragupta Maurya.

questions.question: What is the name of the highest mountain peak in the Eastern Ghats?
questions.explanation: The highest mountain peak in the Eastern Ghats is Mahendragiri, which is located in the state of Odisha. It has an elevation of 1,501 meters (4,925 ft) above sea level.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression?
questions.explanation: Article 19 of the Indian Constitution deals with the Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression. It guarantees the freedom of speech and expression to all citizens of India, subject to certain reasonable restrictions.

questions.question: What is the name of the government scheme that aims to provide financial assistance to small and medium-sized enterprises?
questions.explanation: The Mudra Yojana is a government scheme that aims to provide financial assistance to small and medium-sized enterprises. It was launched in 2015 and provides loans up to Rs. 10 lakh to non-corporate, non-farm sector income-generating activities.

questions.question: What is the process by which water moves through a plant, from the roots to the leaves?
questions.explanation: The process by which water moves through a plant, from the roots to the leaves, is called transpiration. It occurs through the xylem tissue and is an important part of the water cycle.

questions.question: What is the name of the international agreement that aims to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and mitigate climate change?
questions.explanation: The Paris Agreement is an international agreement that aims to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and mitigate climate change. It was adopted in 2015 and has been signed by almost 200 countries.

questions.question: Who is the current Governor of the Reserve Bank of India?
questions.explanation: Shaktikanta Das is the current Governor of the Reserve Bank of India. He took office in 2018 and has been instrumental in implementing various monetary policies to stabilize the Indian economy.

questions.question: The Indian National Congress was founded in which year?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress was founded in 1885 by Allan Octavian Hume, a British civil servant. It was established to promote Indian rights and interests, and later became a major force in the Indian independence movement.

questions.question: What is the name of the largest river island in the world, located in the Brahmaputra River?
questions.explanation: Majuli is the largest river island in the world, located in the Brahmaputra River in the state of Assam. It is approximately 352 square kilometers (136 sq mi) in area and is home to a diverse range of flora and fauna.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the appointment of the Governor of a state?
questions.explanation: Article 155 of the Indian Constitution deals with the appointment of the Governor of a state. It states that the Governor shall be appointed by the President of India, and shall hold office during the pleasure of the President.

questions.question: What is the name of the government scheme that aims to provide housing for all by 2022?
questions.explanation: The Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY) is a government scheme that aims to provide housing for all by 2022. It was launched in 2015 and provides financial assistance to low-income households to build or purchase a house.

questions.question: What is the largest living structure on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Great Barrier Reef is the largest living structure on Earth, spanning more than 2,300 kilometers (1,400 miles) off the coast of Australia. It is home to a diverse range of marine life, including over 1,500 species of fish and 600 species of coral.

questions.question: What is the name of the international agreement that aims to conserve and sustainably use biodiversity?
options: United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change
questions.explanation: The Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD) is an international agreement that aims to conserve and sustainably use biodiversity. It was adopted in 1992 and has been signed by over 190 countries.

questions.question: Who is the current President of the Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: Mallikarjun Kharge is the current President of the Indian National Congress. He took office in 2022 and has been instrumental in leading the party's efforts to revive its fortunes and challenge the ruling Bharatiya Janata Party.

questions.question: The Battle of Panipat was fought between which two armies in 1761?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Panipat was fought between the Mughal and Maratha armies in 1761. It was a decisive victory for the Mughal army, which was led by Ahmad Shah Durrani, and marked the beginning of the end of Maratha power in India.

questions.question: What is the name of the strait that separates the islands of Java and Sumatra?
questions.explanation: The Sunda Strait is the strait that separates the islands of Java and Sumatra in Indonesia. It is approximately 30 kilometers (19 miles) wide and connects the Java Sea to the Indian Ocean.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the powers of the Parliament?
questions.explanation: Article 105 of the Indian Constitution deals with the powers of the Parliament. It states that the Parliament shall have the power to make laws for the whole or any part of the territory of India, and to exercise such other powers and functions as may be conferred upon it by the Constitution.

questions.question: What is the name of the government scheme that aims to provide financial assistance to farmers?
questions.explanation: The Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) is a government scheme that aims to provide financial assistance to farmers. It was launched in 2019 and provides an annual income support of Rs. 6,000 to eligible farmer families.

questions.question: What is the process by which the universe became transparent to light?
questions.explanation: The process by which the universe became transparent to light is called recombination. It occurred around 380,000 years after the Big Bang, when the universe had cooled enough for electrons and protons to combine into neutral atoms, allowing light to travel freely through the universe.

questions.question: What is the name of the international agreement that aims to reduce the production and consumption of ozone-depleting substances?
questions.explanation: The Montreal Protocol is an international agreement that aims to reduce the production and consumption of ozone-depleting substances. It was adopted in 1987 and has been successful in reducing the concentration of ozone-depleting substances in the atmosphere and protecting the ozone layer.

questions.question: Who is the current Chairman of the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO)?
questions.explanation: S. Somanath is the current Chairman of the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO). He took office in 2022 and has been instrumental in leading ISRO's efforts to develop and launch various space missions, including the Gaganyaan mission.

questions.question: The Revolt of 1857 was led by which Indian leader?
questions.explanation: The Revolt of 1857 was led by Bahadur Shah II, the last Mughal emperor of India. He was the nominal leader of the revolt, although the actual leadership was provided by various other Indian leaders, including Mangal Pandey, Tantia Tope, and Rani Lakshmibai.

questions.question: What is the name of the highest mountain peak in the Western Ghats?
questions.explanation: The highest mountain peak in the Western Ghats is Anamudi, which is located in the state of Kerala. It has an elevation of 2,695 meters (8,842 ft) above sea level and is a popular destination for trekking and hiking.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the powers of the President?
questions.explanation: Article 53 of the Indian Constitution deals with the powers of the President. It states that the President shall have the power to appoint the Prime Minister, to summon and prorogue the Parliament, and to grant pardons and reprieves to persons convicted of offenses.

questions.question: What is the name of the government scheme that aims to provide financial assistance to micro, small, and medium enterprises?
questions.explanation: The Mudra Yojana is a government scheme that aims to provide financial assistance to micro, small, and medium enterprises. It was launched in 2015 and provides loans up to Rs. 10 lakh to non-corporate, non-farm sector income-generating activities.

questions.question: What is the process by which plants convert sunlight into energy?
questions.explanation: The process by which plants convert sunlight into energy is called photosynthesis. It occurs in the chloroplasts of plant cells and involves the conversion of carbon dioxide and water into glucose and oxygen, using sunlight as the energy source.

questions.question: What is the name of the international agreement that aims to conserve and manage the world's oceans?
options: United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea
questions.explanation: The United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS) is an international agreement that aims to conserve and manage the world's oceans. It was adopted in 1982 and provides a framework for the use of the world's oceans and their resources.

questions.question: Who is the current Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Narendra Modi is the current Prime Minister of India. He took office in 2014 and has been instrumental in implementing various policies and programs to promote economic growth, social development, and national security.

questions.question: The Taj Mahal was built by which Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal was built by Shah Jahan, the fifth Mughal emperor of India. It was constructed between 1632 and 1653 as a mausoleum for his wife, Mumtaz Mahal, and is considered one of the most beautiful examples of Mughal architecture.

questions.question: What is the name of the largest river in India by discharge?
questions.explanation: The Brahmaputra is the largest river in India by discharge. It originates in the Tibetan Plateau and flows through the states of Arunachal Pradesh and Assam before joining the Ganges River in Bangladesh.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the right to equality?
questions.explanation: Article 14 of the Indian Constitution deals with the right to equality. It states that the state shall not deny to any person equality before the law or the equal protection of the laws within the territory of India.

questions.question: What is the name of the government scheme that aims to provide financial assistance to women?
questions.explanation: The Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana is a government scheme that aims to provide financial assistance to women. It was launched in 2015 and provides a savings scheme for the education and marriage of girls.

questions.question: What is the largest planet in our solar system?
questions.explanation: The largest planet in our solar system is Jupiter. It is a gas giant and has a diameter of approximately 142,984 kilometers (88,846 miles), making it the largest of all the planets in our solar system.

questions.question: What is the name of the international agreement that aims to reduce the use of single-use plastics?
questions.explanation: The United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) is an international agreement that aims to reduce the use of single-use plastics. It was launched in 2018 and provides a framework for countries to reduce their plastic waste and promote sustainable consumption and production patterns.

questions.question: Who is the current Chief Justice of India?
questions.explanation: Dhananjaya Y. Chandrachud is the current Chief Justice of India. He took office in 2022 and has been instrumental in leading the judiciary's efforts to promote justice, equality, and the rule of law in India.

questions.question: The Quit India Movement was launched by which Indian leader in 1942?
questions.explanation: The Quit India Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1942. It was a civil disobedience movement that aimed to end British rule in India and achieve independence through non-violent means.

questions.question: What is the name of the highest mountain peak in the Nilgiri Hills?
questions.explanation: The highest mountain peak in the Nilgiri Hills is Doddabetta, which is located in the state of Tamil Nadu. It has an elevation of 2,637 meters (8,652 ft) above sea level and is a popular tourist destination.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the right to freedom of speech and expression?
questions.explanation: Article 19 of the Indian Constitution deals with the right to freedom of speech and expression. It guarantees the freedom of speech and expression to all citizens of India, subject to certain reasonable restrictions.

questions.question: What is the name of the government scheme that aims to provide financial assistance to small and marginal farmers?
questions.explanation: The Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) is a government scheme that aims to provide financial assistance to small and marginal farmers. It was launched in 2019 and provides an annual income support of Rs. 6,000 to eligible farmer families.

questions.question: What is the process by which the human body converts food into energy?
questions.explanation: The process by which the human body converts food into energy is called cellular respiration. It involves the breakdown of glucose and other nutrients to produce energy, which is then used to power the body's various functions.

questions.question: What is the name of the international agreement that aims to reduce the emission of greenhouse gases?
options: United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change
questions.explanation: The Paris Agreement is an international agreement that aims to reduce the emission of greenhouse gases. It was adopted in 2015 and aims to limit global warming to well below 2 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial levels and pursue efforts to limit it to 1.5 degrees Celsius.

questions.question: Who is the current President of the United States?
questions.explanation: Joe Biden is the current President of the United States. He took office in 2021 and has been instrumental in shaping the country's domestic and foreign policy agenda.

questions.question: The Indian Independence Act was passed by the British Parliament in which year?
questions.explanation: The Indian Independence Act was passed by the British Parliament in 1947. It granted independence to India and Pakistan, and came into effect on August 15, 1947.

questions.question: What is the name of the largest island in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands?
questions.explanation: The largest island in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands is Andaman Island. It is approximately 3,200 square kilometers (1,235 sq mi) in area and is home to a diverse range of flora and fauna.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the right to life and personal liberty?
questions.explanation: Article 21 of the Indian Constitution deals with the right to life and personal liberty. It states that no person shall be deprived of his life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law.

questions.question: What is the name of the government scheme that aims to provide financial assistance to start-ups?
questions.explanation: The Start-up India initiative is a government scheme that aims to provide financial assistance to start-ups. It was launched in 2016 and provides a range of benefits, including funding, tax exemptions, and regulatory support, to start-ups in India.

questions.question: What is the process by which the human body regulates its temperature?
questions.explanation: The process by which the human body regulates its temperature is called thermoregulation. It involves the use of various mechanisms, such as sweating, shivering, and vasodilation, to maintain a stable body temperature.

questions.question: What is the name of the international agreement that aims to protect the ozone layer?
questions.explanation: The Montreal Protocol is an international agreement that aims to protect the ozone layer. It was adopted in 1987 and has been successful in reducing the production and consumption of ozone-depleting substances.

questions.question: Who is the current Chairman of the Tata Group?
questions.explanation: Natarajan Chandrasekaran is the current Chairman of the Tata Group. He took office in 2017 and has been instrumental in shaping the group's strategy and direction.

questions.question: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre occurred in which year?
questions.explanation: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre occurred in 1919. It was a British colonial era massacre that took place on April 13, 1919, in Amritsar, Punjab, and resulted in the deaths of hundreds of unarmed Indian civilians.

questions.question: What is the name of the largest lake in India?
questions.explanation: The largest lake in India is Wular Lake, which is located in the state of Jammu and Kashmir. It is approximately 100 square kilometers (39 sq mi) in area and is an important wetland ecosystem.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the powers of the state legislatures?
questions.explanation: Article 163 of the Indian Constitution deals with the powers of the state legislatures. It states that the state legislatures shall have the power to make laws for the state, subject to the provisions of the Constitution.

questions.question: What is the name of the government scheme that aims to provide financial assistance to rural households?
questions.explanation: The Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Antyodaya Yojana (DAY-NULM) is a government scheme that aims to provide financial assistance to rural households. It was launched in 2014 and provides a range of benefits, including housing, education, and skill training, to rural households.

questions.question: What is the process by which the human body converts glucose into energy?
questions.explanation: The process by which the human body converts glucose into energy is called glycolysis. It is the first step in the breakdown of glucose and involves the conversion of glucose into pyruvate, which is then converted into energy through the citric acid cycle and oxidative phosphorylation.

questions.question: What is the name of the international agreement that aims to protect the world's biodiversity?
questions.explanation: The Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD) is an international agreement that aims to protect the world's biodiversity. It was adopted in 1992 and provides a framework for countries to conserve and sustainably use biodiversity.

questions.question: Who is the current Prime Minister of the United Kingdom?
questions.explanation: Rishi Sunak is the current Prime Minister of the United Kingdom. He took office in 2022 and has been instrumental in shaping the country's economic and social policy agenda.

questions.question: The Sepoy Mutiny of 1857 was also known as which of the following?
questions.explanation: The Sepoy Mutiny of 1857 was also known as the Indian Rebellion of 1857. It was a widespread uprising against British colonial rule in India, and is considered one of the most significant events in Indian history.

questions.question: What is the name of the largest river delta in the world?
questions.explanation: The Ganges-Brahmaputra Delta is the largest river delta in the world, covering an area of approximately 100,000 square kilometers (39,000 sq mi) in India and Bangladesh. It is home to a diverse range of flora and fauna, and is an important agricultural region.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the powers of the High Courts?
questions.explanation: Article 226 of the Indian Constitution deals with the powers of the High Courts. It states that the High Courts shall have the power to issue writs and orders, and to exercise jurisdiction over the subordinate courts in their respective territories.

questions.question: What is the name of the government scheme that aims to promote digital payments in India?
questions.explanation: The Digital India initiative is a government scheme that aims to promote digital payments in India. It was launched in 2015 and provides a range of services, including digital literacy, online education, and digital payment platforms, to promote digital inclusion and financial inclusion in India.
